content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build the Pascal runtime library (pr24p) in Pascal, dogfooding the p24c compiler. Phase 0: hand-written .spc stubs for write_int, write_bool, write_ln. Phase 1: Pascal-compiled standard functions (abs, odd, ord, chr, succ, pred, sqr), bounds/nil checks, and read/readln support. Phase 2: heap management (new/dispose), sets, strings, subrange checks — all in Pascal. Runtime is compiled by p24c into .spc, assembled by pasm into .p24, and runs on pv24a VM (cor24-run emulator or COR24-TD hardware).

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-001-phase0-write-int [production]: Write the hand-written .spc runtime stub for _p24p_write_int. This routine ta...
  [x] 002-002-phase0-write-bool-ln [production]: Add _p24p_write_bool and _p24p_write_ln to src/runtime.spc. write_bool compar...
  [ ] 003-003-phase0-test [production]: Create test .spc programs that exercise all Phase 0 runtime stubs: test write... <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 004-004-phase1-std-functions [production]: Write Phase 1 standard functions in Pascal: abs, odd, ord, chr, succ, pred, s...
  [ ] 005-005-phase1-checks [production]: Write _p24p_bounds_check and _p24p_nil_check in Pascal. bounds_check is calle...
  [ ] 006-006-phase1-read [production]: Write Phase 1 read support in Pascal: _p24p_read_int (reads sign + digits fro...
  [ ] 007-007-phase2-heap [production]: Write heap management in Pascal: _p24p_heap_init (initialize free list), _p24...
  [ ] 008-008-phase2-sets-strings [production]: Write set and string operations in Pascal: set_union, set_intersect, set_diff...
  [ ] 009-009-integration-test [production]: Create end-to-end integration tests: Pascal programs that use runtime routine...

=== CURRENT STEP: 003-003-phase0-test [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: Create test .spc programs that exercise all Phase 0 runtime stubs: test write...
Task type: cor24-asm

=== PROMPT ===
Create test .spc programs that exercise all Phase 0 runtime stubs: test write_int with positive, negative, zero, and boundary values; test write_bool with true and false; test write_ln. Verify output by running through pasm + pv24a VM via cor24-run.

=== PAST SUCCESSES (cor24-asm) ===
  [2026-03-26T10:38:35] action=read pvmasm.s source to understand .proc/.end semantics, two-pass assembler structure, opcode table, VM frame layout. Debugged loada returning 0 (was proc ordering issue). Debugged truncated assembly (512-byte input_buf limit). Created compact test files with short proc/label names., result=success, reward=+1
  [2026-03-26T01:16:37] action=read runtime.md spec, explored pv24a pasm syntax and VM opcodes (enter/leave/call/ret semantics), wrote .spc using stack-machine idiom, created test program, verified assembly syntax matches hello.spc patterns, result=success, reward=+1

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

1→.module runtime
     2→.export _p24p_write_int
     3→.export _p24p_write_bool
     4→.export _p24p_write_ln
     5→
     6→; pr24p — Pascal Runtime Library
     7→; Phase 0: Hand-written .spc stubs for p-code VM syscall wrappers
     8→
     9→; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
    10→; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
    11→; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
    12→; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
    13→; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
    14→; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
    15→.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    16→    loada 0              ; load argument n
    17→    ; check for negative
    18→    dup                  ; n n
    19→    push 0               ; n n 0
    20→    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    21→    jz positive
    22→    ; print minus sign
    23→    push 45              ; n '-'
    24→    sys 1                ; n
    25→    neg                  ; -n
    26→positive:
    27→    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)
    28→    push 0               ; push sentinel
    29→extract:
    30→    loadl 0              ; load n
    31→    push 10
    32→    mod                  ; n % 10
    33→    push 48
    34→    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char
    35→    loadl 0
    36→    push 10
    37→    div                  ; n / 10
    38→    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10
    39→    loadl 0
    40→    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits
    41→wi_print:
    42→    dup
    43→    jz wi_done
    44→    sys 1                ; PUTC digit
    45→    jmp wi_print
    46→wi_done:
    47→    drop                 ; discard sentinel
    48→    ret 1
    49→.end
    50→
    51→; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )
    52→; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.
    53→; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.
    54→; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).
    55→.proc _p24p_write_bool 0
    56→    loada 0              ; load argument b
    57→    jz wb_false
    58→    ; print "TRUE"
    59→    push 84              ; 'T'
    60→    sys 1
    61→    push 82              ; 'R'
    62→    sys 1
    63→    push 85              ; 'U'
    64→    sys 1
    65→    push 69              ; 'E'
    66→    sys 1
    67→    jmp wb_done

    71→    sys 1

    73→    sys 1

    75→    sys 1

    77→    sys 1

    79→    sys 1

    82→.end
    83→
    84→; _p24p_write_ln ( -- )

    86→.proc _p24p_write_ln 0

file.content:
.module runtime
.export _p24p_write_int
.export _p24p_write_bool
.export _p24p_write_ln

; pr24p — Pascal Runtime Library
; Phase 0: Hand-written .spc stubs for p-code VM syscall wrappers

; _p24p_write_int ( n -- )
; Print signed integer to UART as decimal.
; Handles negative numbers (prints '-' prefix).
; Uses div/mod by 10 to extract digits, pushes onto eval stack
; in reverse order with a zero sentinel, then prints from most
; significant to least significant via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_int 1
    loada 0              ; load argument n
    ; check for negative
    dup                  ; n n
    push 0               ; n n 0
    lt                   ; n (n<0?)
    jz positive
    ; print minus sign
    push 45              ; n '-'
    sys 1                ; n
    neg                  ; -n
positive:
    storel 0             ; local0 = abs(n)
    push 0               ; push sentinel
extract:
    loadl 0              ; load n
    push 10
    mod                  ; n % 10
    push 48
    add                  ; n % 10 + '0' = digit char
    loadl 0
    push 10
    div                  ; n / 10
    storel 0             ; update n = n / 10
    loadl 0
    jnz extract          ; if n != 0, extract more digits
wi_print:
    dup
    jz wi_done
    sys 1                ; PUTC digit
    jmp wi_print
wi_done:
    drop                 ; discard sentinel
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_bool ( b -- )
; Print TRUE or FALSE to UART.
; Compares argument to 0: if zero prints FALSE, otherwise prints TRUE.
; Each character is pushed and output via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_bool 0
    loada 0              ; load argument b
    jz wb_false
    ; print "TRUE"
    push 84              ; 'T'
    sys 1
    push 82              ; 'R'
    sys 1
    push 85              ; 'U'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
    jmp wb_done
wb_false:
    ; print "FALSE"
    push 70              ; 'F'
    sys 1
    push 65              ; 'A'
    sys 1
    push 76              ; 'L'
    sys 1
    push 83              ; 'S'
    sys 1
    push 69              ; 'E'
    sys 1
wb_done:
    ret 1
.end

; _p24p_write_ln ( -- )
; Print newline (LF) to UART via sys 1 (PUTC).
.proc _p24p_write_ln 0
    push 10              ; LF
    sys 1
    ret 0
.end

.endmodule

All test cases pass. Output: `42`, `0`, `-5`, `7`, `1`, `-1`, `100`, `999` — covering positive, negative, zero, single-digit, multi-digit, and boundary values.

Now let me commit and complete the step.

input.command:
git add tests/test_write_int.spc tests/test_write_bool_ln.spc && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(phase0): expand write_int test with boundary values (1, -1, 100, 999)

Verified all Phase 0 runtime stubs via pvmasm VM:
- write_int: positive (42, 7, 1, 100, 999), zero (0), negative (-5, -1)
- write_bool: true (1) → TRUE, false (0) → FALSE
- write_ln: newline after each output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
